query_normalized: predicting the impact of cold work on recrystallization kinetics in aluminum alloys materials science
rationale: The paper focuses on the mechanics of cold rolling in **polymeric films**, whereas the user's question specifically concerns **recrystallization kinetics in aluminum alloys** (a metallic system). While both involve "cold work," the underlying physical mechanisms (elastic-plastic deformation in polymers vs. thermally activated recrystallization in metals) and the material domains are distinct, failing the "off-domain entirely" or "distinct construct" rejection criteria.
